methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Seneca
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Rainmaker is a Series A-stage drone and autonomous systems company founded by Augustus Doricko that has raised $25M to date. The broader Drones & Autonomous sector comprises 8 private peers, including Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air, with no public comparables currently available.

The valuation analysis relies on a single applicable method: Stage-Progression. Using total capital raised ($25M) as a proxy for current valuation, a standard 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of progression from Series A to later stages yields an implied valuation of $50M. A Peer-Median approach was not applicable due to insufficient narrow peer comparables at identical or adjacent stages with comparable valuation tiers; while five same-sector peers exist, cross-filtering by stage and scale yielded fewer than three datapoints, making sector-wide median analysis unreliable.

The triangulated valuation range is $42M to $57M, with a median of $50M. This single-method derivation carries material uncertainty inherent in stage-progression multipliers, which can vary based on market conditions, capital efficiency, and technology maturity. The limited peer dataset in Drones & Autonomous—particularly the absence of public comparables—further constrains precision. Key unknowns affecting valuation include Rainmaker's unit economics, revenue traction, and technical differentiation relative to the identified peer set.
